content.problemStatement:
Ayush has two rooted trees. Each of them contains NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN); let's denote a vertex vvv in tree 111 by v1v_1v1‚Äã and a corresponding vertex vvv in tree 222 by v2v_2v2‚Äã.

For each tree ttt, any vertex on the path from the root to a vertex vtv_tvt‚Äã (including vtv_tvt‚Äã) is an ancestor of vtv_tvt‚Äã and the depth of vtv_tvt‚Äã is the distance of vtv_tvt‚Äã from the root of tree ttt.

For any integer www (1‚â§w‚â§N1 \le w \le N1‚â§w‚â§N) and vertices u1u_1u1‚Äã and v2v_2v2‚Äã, www is said to be a common ancestor of u1u_1u1‚Äã and v2v_2v2‚Äã if:


in tree 111, w1w_1w1‚Äã is an ancestor of u1u_1u1‚Äã
in tree 222, w2w_2w2‚Äã is an ancestor of v2v_2v2‚Äã
the depths of vertices w1w_1w1‚Äã and w2w_2w2‚Äã (from their respective roots) are the same
For example, in the figure above, the pair (u1,v2)=(6,1)(u_1, v_2) = (6, 1)(u1‚Äã,v2‚Äã)=(6,1) has two common ancestors ‚Äî 444 and 666. Although vertex 222 is an ancestor for each of them, the depths of 212_121‚Äã and 222_222‚Äã are not the same.

Help Ayush find the maximum number of common ancestors for a pair of vertices, i.e. the largest integer ccc such that there is a pair of vertices (u1,v2)(u_1, v_2)(u1‚Äã,v2‚Äã) (possibly u=vu = vu=v) with ccc common ancestors.
constraints: 1‚â§pi,qi‚â§N1 \leq p_i, q_i \leq N1‚â§pi‚Äã,qi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã describe a tree with exactly one root
q1,q2,‚Ä¶,qNq_1, q_2, \ldots, q_Nq1‚Äã,q2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,qN‚Äã describe a tree with exactly one root
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã. For each valid iii, pip_ipi‚Äã is the parent of the vertex i1i_1i1‚Äã or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if the vertex i1i_1i1‚Äã is the root of tree 111.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers q1,q2,‚Ä¶,qNq_1, q_2, \ldots, q_Nq1‚Äã,q2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,qN‚Äã. Similarly, for each valid iii, qiq_iqi‚Äã is the parent of the vertex i2i_2i2‚Äã or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if the vertex i2i_2i2‚Äã is the root of tree 222.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum number of common ancestors for a pair (u1,v2)(u_1, v_2)(u1‚Äã,v2‚Äã).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is only one pair (u1,v2)=(11,12)(u_1, v_2) = (1_1, 1_2)(u1‚Äã,v2‚Äã)=(11‚Äã,12‚Äã) and only one common ancestor.
Example case 2: If u=1u = 1u=1, then 111 and 222 are common ancestors for any vvv.
Example case 3: The maximum number of common ancestors is 222, e.g. 444 and 666 for (u1,v2)=(6,1)(u_1, v_2) = (6, 1)(u1‚Äã,v2‚Äã)=(6,1), as shown in the figure in the statement.
